claim: Coding Maoist China as uniform sacrificial-escalation obscures significant internal variation: the early PRC (1949-1956) exhibited partial restrained-reordering relative to Nationalist rule, while the Great Leap Forward (1958-1962) and Cultural Revolution (1966-1976) represent escalating sacrificial demands — periodization substantially changes the case's theoretical significance.
rationale: The 1949-1976 period contains multiple distinct order phases; treating them as a unified sacrificial-escalation case conflates the order's different phases and may obscure the internal dynamics by which the CCP moved from partial corrigibility toward frozen pathology and then escalation.
